cubes.title: FastAPI Modular Project Structure
rules: Organize configuration, security, and shared dependencies in a core/ directory
Use models/ for SQLAlchemy database table definitions
Use schemas/ for Pydantic request/response validation models
Place route handlers in api/ with optional versioning (e.g., v1/)
Separate business logic into a services/ directory
Isolate data access logic in a repositories/ directory
cubes.rationale: Improves code maintainability, scalability, and team collaboration by enforcing clear separation of concerns.

cubes.title: Async Python Error Handling
rules: Wrap await calls in try/except blocks to catch coroutine exceptions
Use asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True to prevent task cancellation on failure
Default asyncio.gather behavior cancels pending tasks on the first exception
cubes.rationale: Ensures robust error handling in asynchronous workflows without crashing the event loop or losing task results.

cubes.title: Three-Layer Knowledge Base File Structure
rules: Organize projects into three distinct directories: src/, knowledge_library/, and runtime/
Place all executable code (main.py, routers/, middleware/, models.py) in src/
Store reusable public knowledge in knowledge_library/ under cubes/basic/, cubes/experience/, and anketa/
Keep dynamic, user-specific, or session-based data in runtime/ under personas/, sessions/, and storage/
cubes.rationale: Strictly separates application code, public knowledge, and private runtime data to improve modularity, security, and maintainability.

cubes.title: Manual SSL Certificate Setup for nginx-proxy
rules: Place certificate files in /etc/nginx/certs/ named as <domain>.crt and <domain>.key
Ensure .crt contains the full certificate chain and .key is unencrypted
Disable ssl_reject_handshake or verify cert placement to prevent ERR_SSL_UNRECOGNIZED_NAME_ALERT
cubes.rationale: Ensures proper SSL/TLS termination and prevents handshake failures when automatic certificate generation fails.

cubes.title: Pre-Refactoring File Backup & Organization
rules: Create a timestamped backup of files before modifying them using cp file.py file.py.backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)
Keep only the latest tested version in the active project directory
Move older backups to a dedicated archive/ folder at the project root
Organize archived files into category or module subdirectories
Use version control (Git) alongside manual backups for change tracking
cubes.rationale: Prevents data loss during code refactoring and maintains a clean, easily navigable project directory structure.

cubes.title: Bypass Docker-Compose ContainerConfig KeyError
rules: Forcefully remove the broken container using docker rm -f <container_name_or_id>
Extract required settings (image, ports, volumes, env vars, network) from docker-compose.yml
Recreate the container using docker run with explicit flags (-d, --name, --network, -p, -e, -v)
Explicitly pass every environment variable with -e KEY=VALUE instead of relying on env_file or compose environment blocks
cubes.rationale: Resolves corrupted or incompatible container metadata errors by bypassing docker-compose parsing and directly using the Docker CLI.

cubes.title: Knowledge Cube JSON Loader Implementation
rules: Execute loader function automatically at module import or application startup
Use os.walk() to recursively scan the knowledge_library/ directory for .json files
Handle three JSON formats: single cube object, constitution with a 'cubes' array, and AI-answer with a nested JSON string in the 'answer' field
For files matching cubes_*.json, extract the 'answer' field and parse it using json.loads()
Store parsed cubes in a global list or dedicated in-memory data structure
cubes.rationale: Ensures all knowledge cubes are correctly parsed from multiple JSON formats and loaded into memory automatically at application startup.

cubes.title: Parsing Nested JSON Strings in Data Files
rules: Load outer JSON first using json.load() or json.loads()
Check field type with isinstance(value, str) before parsing
Parse inner strings using json.loads()
Wrap parsing in try/except JSONDecodeError block
Log errors and continue processing on failure
cubes.rationale: Prevents parsing crashes and ensures robust data extraction when handling stringified JSON payloads.

cubes.title: Reflex Cache Architecture
rules: Cache discoveries at the session level to prevent redundant reloading across turns.
Cache search results (brief info) at the dialogue level for reuse within the same conversation.
Implement lazy loading for full cubes; fetch detailed content only when explicitly applied.
Prohibit reloading rules that are already cached to avoid duplicate processing.
cubes.rationale: Minimizes token usage, optimizes response speed, and reduces latency and computational cost.

cubes.title: FastAPI Rate Limiting with SlowAPI
rules: Install slowapi using pip install slowapi
Initialize Limiter with a key function like get_remote_address for IP-based tracking
Attach limiter to app.state.limiter and register the rate limit exceeded handler
Apply decorators or middleware to enforce global or per-endpoint rate limits
cubes.rationale: Protects API availability, prevents resource exhaustion, and ensures fair usage by controlling request frequency.

cubes.title: Qdrant Semantic Search for SKV Cubes
rules: Convert knowledge cubes to dense vector embeddings using a language model
Store embeddings in Qdrant with metadata (ID, title, triggers, category)
Vectorize user queries with the same embedding model
Use cosine similarity for top-k approximate nearest neighbor search
Return only brief cube info initially per SKV Request Efficiency rules
Fetch full cube content only after explicit user selection
Apply blind jury trial and downvote-only rating for quality control
cubes.rationale: Optimizes semantic search performance and API efficiency while ensuring high-quality knowledge delivery through structured vector indexing and community-driven validation.

cubes.title: Prompt Injection Defense Strategies
rules: Adopt a layered, defense-in-depth approach as no single solution is foolproof
Implement strict input validation to reject special characters, code-like syntax, and known injection patterns
Use regex or blocklists to filter common injection phrases, acknowledging they can be bypassed
Restrict maximum input length to limit attack complexity
Decode and normalize user input (e.g., Unicode normalization, URL decoding) before filtering to catch obfuscated attacks
cubes.rationale: Prevents AI systems from executing malicious instructions disguised as user data, maintaining system integrity and security.

cubes.title: LLM Sandbox Pattern for Context Injection
rules: Use the sandbox pattern to isolate untrusted user input from core system instructions
Wrap user input in special delimiters to explicitly mark it as data, not instructions
Apply this technique to prevent prompt injection attacks and unauthorized system prompt overrides
cubes.rationale: Prevents adversaries from embedding malicious instructions in user input that could override system prompts, leak data, or trigger unintended actions.

cubes.title: Persist In-Memory API Data to PostgreSQL
rules: Implement a persistence layer to sync in-memory data with PostgreSQL
Use background workers or scheduled tasks to flush volatile data to the database
Ensure transactional integrity during data migration
cubes.rationale: Bridges fast in-memory storage with durable PostgreSQL databases to maintain performance while ensuring data reliability.

cubes.title: Prevent Silent PostgreSQL Save Failures in Docker
rules: Mount /var/lib/postgresql/data to a persistent Docker volume to prevent data loss on restart
Ensure volume ownership and permissions match the postgres user
Avoid using NFS or VirtualBox shared folders for PostgreSQL data volumes due to fsync incompatibility
Use modern Docker storage drivers like overlay2 to ensure fsync and data integrity
cubes.rationale: Prevents silent data corruption and loss in containerized PostgreSQL by addressing volume mounting, permissions, and filesystem fsync compatibility.

cubes.title: Debugging 403 Forbidden in Docker with urllib
rules: Reproduce the request outside the Docker container to verify if the issue is Docker-specific
If it works on the host but fails in the container, add verbose logging to inspect full request and response headers
Check credentials, API keys, or server access policies if the error persists outside the container
cubes.rationale: Isolates whether the 403 error stems from Docker's network environment (shared IPs, missing headers) or external authentication/policy issues.

cubes.title: Effective Cube Trigger Intent Creation
rules: Use realistic natural language search queries, synonyms, and common variations for trigger phrases
Define clear intent categories (core and contextual) and assign numeric priority levels (1-10) to resolve conflicts
Identify trigger conditions across environmental, entity state, and external event dimensions
Map triggers to intents using structured formats like tables or decision trees
cubes.rationale: Ensures cubes are easily discoverable and accurately map user/system triggers to prioritized intents for reliable execution.

cubes.title: SKV Cube Structure Standard
rules: Include all required fields: cube_id, type, priority, version, title, trigger_intent, rules
Set type to exactly one of: basic, experience, or project
Assign priority as integer 1-4 (1=constitutional, 2=basic, 3=experience, 4=project) where lower numbers override higher in conflicts
Format version using semantic versioning (major.minor.patch)
Provide at least 5 English phrases in trigger_intent for search matching
cubes.rationale: Ensures consistency, proper conflict resolution, and standardized knowledge representation across the SKV system.
